name: Remote System Discovery
description: Adversaries enumerate remote systems within an Azure environment to map the infrastructure for lateral movement and impact planning. In Azure, this manifests as Azure CLI or PowerShell commands listing virtual machines, network interfaces, virtual networks, and subnets. AzureActivity logs these as read operations against MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES, MICROSOFT.NETWORK/VIRTUALNETWORKS, and related resource providers. An adversary with Contributor or Reader role on a subscription can enumerate all reachable systems without any write operations, making the activity appear benign in write-focused alert rules.
iocs: AzureActivity high volume of 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/READ' operations from a single CallerIPAddress in a short window
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.NETWORK/VIRTUALNETWORKS/READ' and 'MICROSOFT.NETWORK/SUBNETS/READ' across multiple resource groups — subnet enumeration
AzureActivity listing all network security groups and their rules: 'MICROSOFT.NETWORK/NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/READ'
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.RESOURCES/SUBSCRIPTIONS/RESOURCEGROUPS/READ' listing all resource groups — full tenant scope enumeration
Sequence of read operations: subscriptions → resource groups → VMs → network interfaces → public IPs — systematic infrastructure mapping
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/INSTANCEVIEW/READ' bulk operation — checking running state of all VMs
AzureActivity CallerIPAddress not matching known DevOps pipeline or admin workstation ranges for resource enumeration operations
evasion_variants: Use Azure Resource Graph queries (MICROSOFT.RESOURCEGRAPH/RESOURCES/READ) which aggregate results in a single request reducing individual operation count
Perform enumeration via the Azure portal rather than CLI/SDK — portal reads may be attributed to the portal infrastructure IP rather than the caller
Enumerate only the target subscription rather than all subscriptions to reduce total read operation volume
Spread enumeration across multiple Azure management API endpoints over several days to stay within normal reader activity baselines
Use a service principal with pre-existing reader access that was used legitimately before compromise — no baseline deviation for that identity
Enumerate via Azure Monitor metrics APIs rather than ARM reads — generates telemetry in different log streams that may lack alerting coverage
detection_kql_hints: AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue has_any ('VIRTUALMACHINES/READ','VIRTUALNETWORKS/READ','NETWORKSECURITYGROUPS/READ') | summarize count() by Caller, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m) | where count_ > 100
AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue has 'READ' | summarize dcount(ResourceGroup) by Caller, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h) | where dcount_ResourceGroup > 10
AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue == 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/INSTANCEVIEW/READ' | summarize count() by CallerIpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m) | where count_ > 20
AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue has 'RESOURCES/SUBSCRIPTIONS/RESOURCEGROUPS/READ' | where not(Caller has 'AzureDevOps') | summarize count() by Caller, CallerIpAddress

AzureActivity | where ActivityStatusValue == 'Success' and OperationNameValue has 'READ' | order by TimeGenerated asc | extend gap = TimeGenerated - prev(TimeGenerated) | where gap < 1s | summarize rapid_reads=count() by Caller
